stem:
Q(t) = 420(1.06)t/2

The function Q models the population, in thousands, of a certain town t years after 2010. According to the model, the population is predicted to increase by n% every 24 months. What is the value of n?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Since 24 months equals 2 years, let x represent the number of 24-month periods. Then t = 2x. Substituting 2x for t in Q(t) = 420(1.06)t/2 gives Q = 420(1.06)x. So every 24 months, the population is multiplied by 1.06, an increase of 6%.

Choice B confuses the multiplier 1.06 with the percent increase. Choice C and Choice D arise from miscomputing the interval scale.
